steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply AA similarity criterion
steps.content: We have $$\angle A = \angle P = 58^{\circ}$$. To prove triangle similarity with minimal additional information, we use the AA criterion—we need just one more pair of equal angles.

steps.title: Evaluate the candidate
steps.content:
Given $$\angle B = 34^{\circ}$$ and $$\angle R = 88^{\circ}$$:

In triangle ABC: $$\angle C = 180^{\circ} - 58^{\circ} - 34^{\circ} = 88^{\circ}$$
In triangle PQR: $$\angle Q = 180^{\circ} - 58^{\circ} - 88^{\circ} = 34^{\circ}$$
Result:
  • $$\angle A = \angle P = 58^{\circ}$$
  • $$\angle B = \angle Q = 34^{\circ}$$
  • $$\angle C = \angle R = 88^{\circ}$$
All three angles match across the correspondence A ↔ P, B ↔ Q, C ↔ R. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.

steps.title: Verify other choices fail
steps.content: The measures of angle B and angle Q are 58 degrees and 34 degrees, respectively. creates angles 58 degrees, 58 degrees, 64 degrees in ABC but 58 degrees, 34 degrees, 88 degrees in PQR—no correspondence exists. Two other options lack the proportionality $$\frac{AB}{AC} = \frac{PQ}{PR}$$ that SAS similarity requires.
